task:
Analyze /Users/vics/dev/structupath/herdr-browser for performance. Context: Herdr terminal plugin; bin/renderer.mjs polls agent-browser for a full-page screenshot + console on an adaptive interval (backs off on idle, caps at 8x), shells out to chafa per frame for ANSI rendering or emits Kitty graphics protocol chunks, parses PNG headers, streams console text, handles SGR mouse input. scripts/*.sh are launch paths. Read all files. Look for: wasted work per poll cycle (e.g. re-fetching unchanged screenshots — is there a hash/ETag skip? re-running chafa on identical frames?), terminal write amplification (full-screen redraw vs diff), base64 chunk sizing for Kitty protocol, JSON parse cost of console tail, spawn overhead of chafa vs keeping it warm, PNG size vs requested viewport, memory growth (console buffer, image cache), and wakeups while pane is unfocused. Report concrete, measured-reasoning findings (P0/P1/P2) with file:line and the expected win. Do NOT edit files — report only.

## Acceptance Contract
Acceptance level: attested
Completion is not accepted from prose alone. End with a structured acceptance report.

Criteria:
- criterion-1: Return concrete findings with file paths and severity when applicable

Required evidence: review-findings, residual-risks

Finish with a fenced JSON block tagged `acceptance-report` in this shape:
Use empty arrays when no items apply; array fields contain strings unless object entries are shown.
`criteriaSatisfied[].status` must be exactly one of: satisfied, not-satisfied, not-applicable.
`commandsRun[].result` must be exactly one of: passed, failed, not-run.
`manualNotes` and `notes` are optional strings; an empty string means no note and does not satisfy `manual-notes` evidence.
```acceptance-report
{
  "criteriaSatisfied": [
    {
      "id": "criterion-1",
      "status": "satisfied",
      "evidence": "specific proof"
    }
  ],
  "changedFiles": [
    "src/file.ts"
  ],
  "testsAddedOrUpdated": [
    "test/file.test.ts"
  ],
  "commandsRun": [
    {
      "command": "command",
      "result": "passed",
      "summary": "short result"
    }
  ],
  "validationOutput": [
    "validation output or concise summary"
  ],
  "residualRisks": [
    "none"
  ],
  "noStagedFiles": true,
  "diffSummary": "short description of the diff",
  "reviewFindings": [
    "blocker: file.ts:12 - issue found, or no blockers"
  ],
  "manualNotes": "anything else the parent should know"
}
```

criteria.must: Return concrete findings with file paths and severity when applicable
Return concrete findings with file paths and severity when applicable

criteriaSatisfied.evidence: Returned 1 P0, 4 P1, 5 P2 findings, each with file:line (e.g. bin/renderer.mjs:129-143 missing maxBuffer; :318-327 post-capture hash skip; :250-262 chafa kitty 3.6MB frames; :536-538 no unfocused suspension), plus measured baselines (agent-browser spawn 4-6ms warm — compiled binary; chafa 19-32ms, kitty output 1.6-3.6MB; JSON.parse 2ms/2.9MB) and explicit verified-non-issues (hash-skip exists at :320-327, consoleLines capped at :285-286, backoff at :87-90).

commandsRun.summary: Confirmed: screenshot defaults to viewport-only (no --full in plugin); console has no incremental cursor (only --clear); batch --json exists (multi-command in one spawn); stream enable provides WebSocket/CDP-screencast push

commandsRun.command: time agent-browser session list --json (x3); file $(command -v agent-browser)
commandsRun.summary: 4-6ms warm per CLI invocation; agent-browser is a compiled Mach-O arm64 binary, not a node cold start

commandsRun.command: chafa -f kitty|symbols -s 80x20/120x30 --animate off --probe off spike-out/test.png | wc -c; timed
commandsRun.summary: kitty: 1.64MB @80x20, 3.63MB @120x30, 32ms; symbols: 5.1KB/11KB, 19ms — quantifies kitty write amplification

commandsRun.command: node -e JSON.parse benchmark of 2.9MB console payload
commandsRun.summary: 2ms parse — console JSON parse cost is a non-issue; the 1MiB execFile default maxBuffer is the real console risk

validationOutput: Read bin/renderer.mjs (551 lines) in full, all 6 scripts/*.sh, herdr-plugin.toml, package.json, README.md, and all 3 test files; report-only task, no files edited
grep-verified every cited line number against bin/renderer.mjs
residualRisks: P0 maxBuffer overflow is inferred from Node's documented 1MiB default + unbounded console text length; not reproduced against a live daemon (reproducing would create a browser session on this machine)
P1-1 screencast streaming via 'agent-browser stream enable' is verified to exist in CLI help, but its exact WebSocket frame protocol was not inspected; integration effort is an estimate
P1-3 kitty f=100 PNG passthrough is confirmed for Kitty/Ghostty by protocol spec; WezTerm support needs verification before removing the chafa kitty path
P1-4 assumes herdr 0.7.x may not expose pane visibility to the pane process; if it does, suspension is cleaner than longer backoff tiers
childReport.diffSummary: No diff — read-only performance analysis; zero files modified
reviewFindings: P0: bin/renderer.mjs:129-143 - execFile on agent-browser console fetch uses default 1MiB maxBuffer; a >1MiB 1000-entry console ring makes every tick throw, freezing image+console and burning 5 spawns/tick forever under a misleading 'not responding' banner

P1: bin/renderer.mjs:318-327 - screenshot is captured/PNG-encoded/written/read/hashed every tick even on static pages; hash-skip happens after all expensive work; agent-browser 'stream enable' (CDP screencast push) is the correct fix
P1: bin/renderer.mjs:306-319 - 4 CLI spawns per tick; verified 'agent-browser batch --json' collapses to 1 spawn (~75% fewer spawns, one JSON parse)

P1: bin/renderer.mjs:250-262 - chafa -f kitty emits full-res RGBA: measured 3.63MB/frame @120x30 from a 15KB source PNG; hand-rolled kitty f=100 PNG passthrough (4096-byte base64 chunks) cuts TTY bytes ~3-10x and drops the 20-35ms chafa spawn
P1: bin/renderer.mjs:536-538 - no unfocused-pane suspension; idle floor is 4-5 spawns + full screenshot every 8s for the pane's lifetime
P2: bin/renderer.mjs:319 - screenshot serialized after metadata Promise.all instead of batched/parallel (~10-40ms wasted tick latency)
P2: bin/renderer.mjs:344,265-273 - header() rewritten every tick and renderConsole full-region redraw per entry batch instead of scroll-append
P2: bin/renderer.mjs:255-261 - chafa stdout buffered to 32MB then single write; stream to stdout instead (moot if kitty f=100 lands)
P2: bin/renderer.mjs:152,479 - JPEG screenshots would shrink the tick's disk/hash chain ~50-80% but require a JPEG SOF dims parser for click mapping

verified-good: hash-skip exists (renderer.mjs:320-327), memory bounded (consoleLines cap :285-286), backoff present (:87-90), console JSON parse ~2ms worst case, scripts/*.sh have no steady-state costs
childReport.manualNotes: Key grounding facts discovered on this machine: agent-browser 0.28 is a compiled binary (spawn ~5ms, so spawn overhead is a smaller issue than assumed — the daemon-side PNG encode and the kitty frame size are the real per-tick costs); agent-browser already ships 'batch --json' and 'stream enable' (WebSocket/CDP screencast), making P1-2 a small change and P1-1 feasible without new dependencies. Highest leverage ordering: P0 one-liner -> P1-2 batch -> P1-3 kitty f=100.
